_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Designing the Phase 5 implementation. Starting with the domain model.

The NationalRegisterPerson entity will hold names, birth dates, nationality, optional BIS and national register numbers, and a register status. Value objects will validate BIS and national register number formats. Person will also be enhanced.

Person gains a nullable linked register ID, BIS and national register numbers, and a link status. Methods will create persons from register records, assign BIS numbers, convert to national register numbers, and link to register stubs. RegistrationCase will also be enhanced.

RegistrationCase will gain `LinkExistingPerson` to reuse existing register stubs instead of creating new persons. `RecordIdentity` will remain for new person creation, but now includes a national register search step before identity recording. A duplicate detection flag may also be added to the case.

The registration flow now has clear steps. Officers open a case and search the national register before recording identity. Matched persons can be linked directly or a new person created instead. BIS-only persons can be converted to national register numbers later. The duplicate detection banner appears when identity is recorded without searching or when potential matches are ignored.

Linking a register record creates a Person entity and sets its ID on the case. Identity is marked as established. National register numbers must be unique across active cases. A test expects rejection when a number is already assigned elsewhere. Checking RegistrationCase for existing national register fields.

Mapping out the NationalRegister domain files: entities, value objects, repository interface, search criteria, matches, and scoring logic. The search service will use simple fuzzy matching rules to score potential duplicates.

Person methods will be named `CreateFromNationalRegister` and `ConvertBisToNationalRegister`. The latter converts a BIS number to a national register number only when the person has a BIS and no national register number yet. `LinkExistingPerson` will accept an optional `IPersonAlreadyLinkedChecker` dependency. A `PossibleDuplicateWarning` flag on the case will trigger the duplicate banner. The `PersonDto` will carry a `NationalRegisterLink` object containing the BIS or national register number and a flag indicating whether the possible duplicate warning was dismissed.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Phase 5 implementation is starting now.

The domain layer structure is taking shape. It includes a person entity with identity and national register numbers, value objects for BIS and national register numbers, a repository interface, search criteria, match results with scoring, and a static search scorer.

A domain exception will handle cases where a person is already linked to a national register record.

The Person entity is gaining fields for national register identifiers and BIS numbers, plus methods to create a person from a register record and convert a BIS number to a national register number.

RegistrationCase is also being updated with a flag to track dismissed duplicate banners and a reference to the linked national register person.

The `LinkExistingPerson` method will be placed on the Person entity rather than the registration case. It validates the intake status, prevents linking an already-linked person, creates a new person from the register record, assigns the ID, and marks identity as established. National register assignment checks will remain in the handler.

The `ConvertBisToNationalRegister` method will validate that a BIS number exists and no national register number is assigned yet, then set the national register number. Both numbers may be retained for audit purposes. Updating the national register person stub record is optional for this phase. The person repository will also gain an asynchronous method to check whether a national register number is already assigned.

The repository interface will also include an asynchronous check to see if a national register record is already linked.

Infrastructure work covers database configuration, a search-enabled repository, a seeder, and a migration for the new table and person columns.

Web features will include a search endpoint with handler, request, response, and validator, plus a link-existing-person endpoint with its own handler, request, response, and validator.

The CreateNewPerson slice may be skipped in favor of RecordIdentity with an optional duplicate acknowledgment flag.

A ConvertBisNumber endpoint will generate a new national register number from the birth date and a five-digit sequence, or accept one provided in the request. The educational stub will use a simplified eleven-digit format with a mod97 check.

A stub generator will build national register numbers from the person’s birth date and a three-digit sequence. The ConvertBisNumber handler may instead select the next available number.

The UI will add a NationalRegisterSearchDialog containing a search form, a scored results table, and action buttons for linking or creating a new person. The identity step will also gain a search button before the identity form.

After identity is linked or created, the UI will display BIS and national register information, a duplicate warning banner, and a convert BIS button when applicable. Rather than extracting a full IdentityStep component, the logic will be added directly to RegistrationCaseDetail or placed under Registration/Components to minimize refactoring scope. The concrete UI pieces are NationalRegisterSearchDialog, NationalRegisterDuplicateBanner, and the updated RegistrationCaseDetail. Seed data examples are also being prepared.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Implementing Phase 5 systematically: domain model, infrastructure, API slices, UI, tests, and documentation.

_**Agent (default)**_

Phase 5 — **National Register search & BIS** is implemented. All **87 tests** pass.

## What was built

### Domain
- `NationalRegisterPerson` stub entity with `BisNumber` and `NationalRegisterNumber` value objects
- `NationalRegisterSearchScorer` — fuzzy matching (100% exact, 80% same DOB + similar name, 50% name-only)
- `Person.CreateFromRegisterRecord()` and `ConvertBisToNationalRegister()`
- `RegistrationCase.LinkExistingPerson()` alongside existing `RecordIdentity()`

### Infrastructure
- `national_register_persons` table + person NR/BIS columns (EF migration `NationalRegisterSearch`)
- Seeded demo records: Marie Leclerc (BIS), Amélie Bernard (duplicate), Jean Dupont (NR), J. Dupont (fuzzy), Sofia Nguyen (control)

### API slices
| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `GET /api/registration/national-register/search` | Scored search |
| `POST /api/registration/cases/{id}/identity/link` | Link existing register record |
| `POST /api/registration/cases/{id}/identity/convert-bis` | BIS → NR conversion (stub generator) |

### UI (case detail)
- **Search National Register** button + dialog with scored results and **Link** action
- **Possible duplicate** warning banner when identity was created without linking but matches exist
- BIS/NR display on identity card + **Convert BIS to NR** button when applicable

## Demo flow

1. Open a new case → **Search National Register** → **Marie Leclerc / 1975-01-01** → Link
2. Identity shows BIS number → **Convert BIS to National Register number**
3. Or record **Amélie Bernard / 1992-03-20** manually → duplicate warning appears

Full notes: [docs/phases/phase-5-national-register-search-bis.md](docs/phases/phase-5-national-register-search-bis.md)

If AppHost is already running, restart it so the new migration applies on PostgreSQL. SQLite/in-memory tests pick up the schema automatically.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The search criteria will be made optional. This allows searching by name alone and selecting from multiple results.

The scorer will require at least one criterion and support partial name matching. A given name like "Marie" will match candidates starting with or containing that name. The request, validator, and handler will also be updated.

The dialog UI will drop the required field indicator and allow partial searches. Tests will be updated accordingly. Partial name matches will score below the full name threshold to ensure multiple candidates appear. Searching for "Marie" will return Marie Leclerc and any other matching records.

Partial name matches will score 40 points. Exact birth dates will score 50. Multiple criteria will combine their scores. A single partial name match will need a minimum score of 30.

Partial name matches will score 45 points. The minimum reportable score will be lowered to 40 so partial results appear. A helper method is being added to handle partial name matching.

The partial name matching helper will use ordinal string comparison against normalized names. The score logic now checks which criteria are present and applies exact matching when all three fields are provided, while treating the birth date as optional. A single partial given name match triggers the corresponding score path.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Nullable DateOnly fields may require optional query binding in the API endpoint.

The existing Link buttons already allow selecting from multiple search results. The scorer will use optional criteria, with a HasAnyCriterion check to ensure at least one search field is provided.

Scoring logic now treats missing criteria as unknown rather than failing. A perfect score of 100 requires all provided fields to match exactly.

Scoring tiers are now defined. Eighty points require a matching birth date plus partial name similarity. Fifty points apply when names match but birth does not. Forty points apply when only one name field is provided and partially matched. The ScoreCandidate method is being refactored to evaluate these conditions cleanly.

Exact and partial name comparisons are now handled separately. The scorer returns zero if any provided name criterion fails its partial match.

The scorer now returns zero when a provided birth date does not match. Exact name and date of birth combinations yield 100 points. Matching birth date with partial name similarity yields 80 points. Exact name matches alone yield 50 points.

The scorer now handles partial matches for individual name fields alone. It also scores cases where only the date of birth matches. Combined partial matches between given name and birth date yield sixty points.

The scorer now also awards sixty points when family name and date of birth partially match. A fallback forty-point score applies to any remaining partial match.

Searching for "Marie" alone will return candidates whose given name partially matches, such as Marie Leclerc. Searching for "Dupont" will return both Jean Dupont and J. Dupont through partial family name matching.

The partial name matcher will normalize input and check for substring or prefix matches. Accent-insensitive comparison is also under consideration.

Case-insensitive matching will handle accents for now. The API request record uses nullable strings and DateOnly for the search fields. Validation ensures at least one criterion is provided, and any supplied birth date must be in the past.
</details></think>

Making search criteria optional and adding partial-name matching so queries like "Marie" return multiple results.

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — that’s in place now.

**What changed**

- All search fields are **optional** — you need at least one of given name, family name, or date of birth.
- **Partial matching** works: typing `Marie` returns Marie Leclerc; typing `Dupont` returns both Jean and J. Dupont.
- The dialog no longer marks fields as required, and the date picker is clearable.

**How it works**

| Search | Results |
|--------|---------|
| `Marie` | Marie Leclerc |
| `Dupont` | Jean Dupont + J. Dupont |
| Full name + DOB | Higher score (100%) |

Each row still has a **Link** button to select that person. Try **Search National Register** with just `Marie` — you should see the match list immediately.

---

Documentation for Phase 5 and the partial-search enhancement is expanded across the docs tree.

## New feature docs

| Document | Content |
|----------|---------|
| [search-national-register.md](docs/features/registration/search-national-register.md) | Partial search, scoring, API examples, dialog behaviour |
| [link-existing-person.md](docs/features/registration/link-existing-person.md) | Link flow, conflicts, vs create-new |
| [convert-bis-number.md](docs/features/registration/convert-bis-number.md) | BIS → NR conversion, stub generator, UI |

## Expanded phase doc

[phase-5-national-register-search-bis.md](docs/phases/phase-5-national-register-search-bis.md) now includes:

- Architecture diagram and identity paths (search → link / create / convert)
- Full scoring table with partial-search examples (`Marie`, `Dupont`, etc.)
- Domain model changes (`Person` NR fields, conflicts)
- Seed data reference with stable IDs
- Four demo walkthroughs (partial search, exact, duplicate warning, multiple Duponts)
- UI component map and test coverage (90 tests)

## Updated cross-references

- [registration/README.md](docs/features/registration/README.md) — NR workflow diagram, slice map, Person ER fields
- [get-registration-case.md](docs/features/registration/get-registration-case.md) — `possibleDuplicateMatches`, extended `PersonDto`
- [record-identity.md](docs/features/registration/record-identity.md) — search-first workflow vs create-new
- [features/README.md](docs/features/README.md) — Phase 5 slice index
